content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 3, 1990

Ms. Betty Doubravsky
2523 Adams Street
Riverside, CA 92504

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-396

Dear Ms. Doubravsky:

You have requested advice on behalf of "Riverside Tomorrow"
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

You are the former treasurer of a city general purpose
committee called "Riverside Tomorrow," which was formed to address
local land use issues and was not controlled by a candidate or
officeholder. The Committee terminated in December 1989 with an
outstanding debt of approximately $1,000. The Committee recently
received a check from the State Compensation Insurance Fund in the
amount of $378. In a telephone conversation on June 19, 1990, you
indicated a desire to endorse the check over to the business
entity to which the outstanding debt is owed.

QUESTIONS

1. May you endorse the check over to the business entity to
which an outstanding debt is owed?

2. What reporting obligations will the Committee incur when
it makes the payment?

CONCLUSIONS

1. The Committee may endorse the check over to the business
entity.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

2.     The Committee must file a semi-annual campaign statement
(Form 420) reporting receipt of the State Fund check, the payment
on the outstanding debt, and any other campaign activity which
occurred during the period covered by the semi-annual statement.

ANALYSIS

     The Act does not prohibit a non-controlled committee which
has closed its campaign bank account from endorsing a check over
to a business entity to which it owes money.  However, committees
are required to file semi-annual campaign statements disclosing
such transactions.  (Section 84200.) A semi-annual campaign
statement will be triggered whenever a terminated committee
receives funds to make a payment to reduce its outstanding debt.
(Ross Advice Letter, No. A-88-225, copy enclosed.)  Please note,
the Committee should keep in its records a copy of the State Fund
check and should include on the campaign statement a note
explaining that receipt of the check was unexpected.

     For your convenience I have enclosed a campaign disclosure
statement (Form 420) and the 1990 Information Manual C--Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act For: General
Purpose Recipient Committees.

     Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions concerning this matter.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

BY: Lynda Cassady
    Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

2. What reporting obligations will the Committee incur when it makes the payment?
